Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the core features and functions of SAP Asset Performance Management (APM).

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to understand and configure core functionality and integration aspects of SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) and learn how with SAP Asset Performance Management asset centric organizations can close the loop between equipment strategy, planning and execution by linking the entire process with a single digital thread.

entry.title: Re: Get all orders for a specific SoldToParty via API (A2X)
entry.content:
I tend to use the eq, ne, lt etc. operators to differ better from the URL parameters that are set. 

You can use the datetime formatter to send a date value and the date range has to be calculated as two dates accordingly

https://<your-host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder?$top=20&$filter=SoldToParty eq '229670' and CreationDate ge datetime'2024-01-31T01:00:00' and CreationDate le datetime'2024-02-03T00:00:00' &$inlinecount=allpages&$orderby=SalesOrder

entry.title: Re: EC UI is displaying the Option ID from a picklist ins...
entry.content:
The Problem is in Picklist for Label Translation should be a text. If there is no Text for Example for EN, then they see just the OptionId. 
You see the right one, because in Proxy Language is not changed to user's Language and you see system in your preferred Language in Settings. 

Best Scenario is to change your language to user's language and proxy. You can use Google Translate to use the Transactions. 

So, Fix the translation in Picklist and they will see the correct Texts too.

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing - backflushing components
entry.content:
Hi,
Showing backflush postings in SFC Report is a known gap / roadmap item.
DM does not post any special message for backflush indeed, but you should find the goods issue posting for backflush items inside Process Order Quantity Confirmation message at to_ProcOrdConfMatlDocItm segment.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: What are the steps to follow S/4HANA CLOUD PUBLIC to...
entry.content:
@xabiel ,

Thanks, Does that mean do you want to create own ODATA API with Post Method ? If that's the case - i am afraid in Public cloud it is not possible to have you ODATA API with Post Method. However you can have a custom ODATA API via released CDS Views (with Read Method) - Not related to your case. 

In Public Cloud, we need to use the whitelisted artefacts provided by SAP like API's/CDS View and Business Objects. 

Did you get a chance to look at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to see if there are API with Post method for your business case ?

Hope this helps, otherwise please let us know. Thanks

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: Re: material ***** in plant **** already has a marked sta...
entry.content:
Dear @mcelik1,

First of all, You need to delete marked cost estimate before create a new one.

1. Use CKR1 tcode and enter material & plant information. You can check if the cost estimate by selecting it and delete it.

2. Create a new cost estimate with CK11N. Enter material, plant and costing variant and create new cost estimate.

3. CK24 tcode could help you to mark the cost estimate. Then, release the cost estimate with CK40N tcode.

entry.title: Shape the future of Simplified Implementation Experience in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
**Join Our Feedback Project: Shape the Future of Simplified Implementation Experience in SAP Cloud ALM! **

We are excited to announce the launch of our feedback project for the "Simplified Implementation Experience," a new application designed to revolutionize and simplify task management in SAP Cloud ALM. We invite you to be part of this initiative and help us create a tool that perfectly meets your needs.

About Simplified Implementation Experience

Simplified Implementation Experience aims to streamline task execution for project teams by incorporating intuitive features and a user-friendly interface. It seeks to simplify the complexities of project management, enabling seamless collaboration and efficient progress tracking across various phases.

Key Features:

  • Phase-Based Task Organization: Organize tasks according to different project phases, making it easier to manage and track progress.
  • Seamless Integration with External Applications: Connect with other tools and applications you use daily for a smooth workflow.
  • Real-Time Progress Monitoring: Keep track of your project's progress in real-time, ensuring you stay on top of every task.
  • Intuitive Task Navigation: Navigate through tasks effortlessly with a user-friendly interface designed for efficiency.
  • Focused Task Display: Focus on what matters most with a clear display of tasks, prioritizing what needs immediate attention.
  • Task Execution Status Setting: Easily set and update the status of task execution, providing clear visibility into your project's advancement.

Why Your Feedback Matters

We believe that the best products are built by listening to the people who use them. Your insights and experiences are invaluable to us. By participating in this feedback project, you'll have the opportunity to influence the development of Simplified Implementation Experience and ensure it meets your expectations and requirements.

How to Get Involved

Registering for our feedback project is simple. Follow these steps to get started:

  1. Sign Up: Click the link to sign up and register.
  2. Provide Feedback: Participate in the calls and express your thoughts, suggestions, and any improvement requests.
  3. Stay Engaged: Participate in regular feedback sessions and surveys to help us refine and improve the application.

Benefits of Participating

  • Early Access: Be among the first to experience and use the Simplified Implementation Experience.
  • Influence Development: Your feedback will directly impact the features and functionalities of the application.
  • Exclusive Updates: Receive regular updates on the progress and new features of the application.
  • Community Engagement: Connect with other users and share insights and best practices.

Sign Up Now, Registration needs to be completed by July 11th, 2024.

Help us create the ultimate tool that simplifies and enhances your capabilities. Register now to be part of the Simplified Implementation Experience feedback project and shape the future of project management.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3722

Together, let's create something amazing!

entry.title: Re: SAP BO - What are the possible reasons for 'no data t...
entry.content:
It means that the query that WebI executed in the back-end database did not return any rows.  But that can happen for any number of reasons, including:

  • The conditions in the query simply don't match any records in the database.  
  • The query included a prompt, and the value entered for the prompt is not valid or does not exist in the database
  • The query contains an illogical combination of conditions (ex., "Region Equals 'East' And Region Equals 'West'"
  • Objects selected in the query (from results and/or conditions) result in tables being combined that weren't intended to be in the same query
  • Row-level security is in use, and the person executing the query does not have sufficient permissions
  • Passthrough authentication is used, and the person executing the query does not have sufficient permissions in the database
  • One or more tables in the database are empty

entry.title: Re: Leveraging Generative AI Advancements for Linked Business Data
entry.content:
Hi Johannes, this is very interesting and very different from the approach we followed on our side for Self-supervised learning based on tabular data.

We studied 3 angles;

  • Possibility to apply Fine Tuning to an existing model based on Tabular Data
  • Possibility to use Attention Mechanism and Transformer Architecture based on tabular data (your study)
  • Possibility to apply In Context Learning to existing LLM

Although In Context Learning is faster to implement and cheaper since we dont update the model parameters, we heavily rely on the quality and relevance of the examples provided in the context, making it very sensitive to the order and the curated of the data we provide in context to the model, and the model we used.

Fine Tuning provided much better results, higher accuracy and control even though the datasets must be curated meticulously, as its often said, the quality of the data is what matters and we are Ok to limit ourseleves not to continously pre-train the models and live with past experiences.

we paused our architecture research when we realized that probably the best architecture was old Gradient Boosted Decision Trees (GBDTs), like XGBoost, because they were less computationally expensive and require less hyperparameter tuning compared to deep learning models.

So keep it coming! I will love seeing SAP progress on XTab and how it scores in TabZilla and similar benchmarks, or even better, with an SAP table specific benchmark.

entry.title: How to Create a Custom Validation from Fiori App Manage Substitution and Validation Rules
entry.content:
As we are moving to Fiori first yet cloud ready approach for any development, why restrict us to GGB0 or GGB1 for creating validation or substitution rules?

We can utilize the Fiori application - Manage Substitution and Validation Rules to create the rules which will be applied to all the standard processes such as standard interfaces, GUI T-codes and managing standard processes in custom program.

In this blog, we will create a custom-defined function in the validation rule on asset creation or modification done through the Fiori app - ‘Manage Fixed Assets’. The rule will validate against the supplier assigned to the asset master record.

Note that the same validation will work through any means of creating or modifying asset master data as mentioned earlier.

Steps to create a custom validation rule

1. Open the Fiori app – “Manage Substitution and Validation Rules” and click on ‘Create Rule’.

2. Enter Business Context, Event, select Rule Type and click on Create.

3. Enter the Rule Name and Description. Decide what should happen post validation in control level. You could give a warning or an error, along with a message defined in the message class assigned here.

4. If there are preconditions, you can assign them. But in this blog, we will create only a validation by clicking on F4 help.

5. Upon clicking the help, we get all the CDS entities list with respect to the business context chosen when we created the rule in step 2. But we also get a list of functions which we can utilize to create our validation rule. Based on our requirement, we can also extend the CDS views from the list through the Fiori app – “Custom Fields and Logic” and we can also create a custom function rather than relying on the four functions we see in the list above.

6. In this demonstration, we will create a customer-defined function and use it for our validation. For that, we will create a class by implementing the interface - IF_FIN_RE_CUSTOM_FUNCTION.
When we implement this interface, it allows us to integrate custom ABAP logic into the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules app. Once the implementing ABAP class is activated, the implementation is made available as a function inside the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules app in the functions list.

To implement the required methods, you can take help from the SAP provided reference class - CL_SUBVAL_WEEKDAY. The class documents the functionality of each method with logic.

Below is the brief explanation of each method -

IS_DISABLED: Remove the method for visibility in all events or use IV_EVENT_ID for specific event visibility in Manage Substitution and Validation Rules app.
GET_NAME: Returns the name of the customer-defined function
GET_RETURNTYPE: Define the ABAP type for the return value of IF_FIN_RE_CUSTOM_FUNCTION~EXECUTE method. Update the return type based on your needs.
GET_RETURN_VALUEHELP: Returning a CDS view for value help and a field for the function's return value.
GET_PARAMETERS: Returns a list of function parameters with unique names, specifying technical ABAP types.
GET_DESCRIPTION: Return a message ID/No. for the text description of customer-defined function that is to be displayed in the functions list.
CHECK_AT_RULE_ACTIVATION: Implement this method to enforce checks on parameters passed to a substitution/validation rule, ensuring only specified types or values are accepted for activation.
EXECUTE: Method to actually implement the logic of the function at hand.

Below is the code for the class defined -

 

CLASS zcl_test_asset_val DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_fin_re_custom_function .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    TYPES:    gty_country TYPE c LENGTH 2.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_test_asset_val IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~execute.

*   Data declaration
    DATA lv_supplier TYPE c LENGTH 10.

*   Get supplier from parameters
    lv_supplier = is_runtime-parameters[ 1 ]-value->*.

*   Get supplier's country
    SELECT SINGLE FROM i_supplier WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
      FIELDS Country WHERE Supplier = _supplier INTO (lv_country).

*   Pass space if country is DE, else 'X'
    IF lv_country EQ 'DE'.
      ev_result = REF #( abap_false ).
    ELSE.
      ev_result = REF #( abap_true ).
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_description.

    rs_msg = VALUE symsg( msgid = 'ZTEST' msgno = '000' ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_name.

    rv_name = 'ZCHECK_ASSET'.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_parameters.

    rt_parameters = VALUE #( ( name = 'SUPPLIER' ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_fin_re_custom_function~get_returntype.

    ro_type = CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( 'ZCL_TEST_ASSET_VAL=>GTY_COUNTRY' ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.​​

 

 

7. Once the implementing class is activated, we can see the custom function appearing in the list.
8. Once the function is selected, it will pop-up the importing parameters that we have specified in the implementing class’s method GET_PARAMETERS. We can assign the value of parameter as a constant, a field from available CDS views, or another function.

9. We will select supplier from the CDS view - C_ASSETTP and click on save.

10. In our logic we would validate the supplier country. If it is ‘DE’, we pass nothing and the validation is passed, else we pass ABAP_TRUE and its failed.

 

 

Now, save the rule. That’s it. Now, we can go to the Fiori app – “Manage Fixed Assets” and test the validation. Upon saving asset, we get below error if country of supplier is not DE.

 Notes & Lessons:

  1. The facility to create rules from Fiori app is available from SAP S/4HANA 2020.
  2. The facility to create a customer-defined function is available from SAP S/4HANA 2022.
  3. Never keep the implementing class inactive which implements the interface IF_FIN_RE_CUSTOM_FUNCTION. Always remember to activate the class without any errors or incorrect datatype assignments, otherwise it would break the Fiori app - “Manage Substitution and Validation Rules”.
  4. Never create more than one implementing class implementing the interface IF_FIN_RE_CUSTOM_FUNCTION having same customer-defined function. If class A and class B, both are returning the same function name then Fiori app - “Manage Substitution and Validation Rules” would break.
  5. In the method GET_PARAMETERS of the interface IF_FIN_RE_CUSTOM_FUNCTION, make sure to follow below rules –
    1. Do not keep space in the name of parameter.
    2. Keep the order of parameters same when assigning the CDS view’s field to the customer-defined function and when passing it back in this method.
  6. Message class used at control level during rule creation must not be assigned to the local package. It will not reflect in the value help while creating the rule if assigned to the local package.
  7. Try keeping the implementing class in ABAP Language Version - 'ABAP for Cloud Development' to keep the core clean and cloud ready.
  8. The validation or substitution created from Fiori also works through standard interfaces and GUI transactions.

Hello

This is a system standard behavior, check the information provided in KBA 3083974 for more details:

'The discontinued part for subcontracting requirements is not supported in the standard system.
If a requirement laying after the effective-out date is not covered, it will have exception message 58 "Uncovered requirement outside of effective-out date" in the MRP list (MD05).
This requirement will not be covered by any new order proposals.'

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3083974

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: saplyphtesttest create a alv
entry.content:
REPORT z_alv_example.

" 数据声明
DATA: gt_data TYPE TABLE OF spfli,
gs_data TYPE spfli.

" ALV相关声明
DATA: gt_fieldcat TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
gs_fieldcat TYPE lvc_s_fcat,
gt_layout TYPE lvc_s_layo,
gt_events TYPE slis_t_event,
gs_event TYPE slis_alv_event.

" 数据选择
SELECT * FROM spfli INTO TABLE gt_data.

" 准备字段目录
PERFORM prepare_fieldcat USING 'CARRID' '航空公司' 10.
PERFORM prepare_fieldcat USING 'CONNID' '航班连接编号' 10.
PERFORM prepare_fieldcat USING 'CITYFROM' '出发城市' 20.
PERFORM prepare_fieldcat USING 'CITYTO' '目的城市' 20.
PERFORM prepare_fieldcat USING 'AIRPFROM' '出发机场' 20.
PERFORM prepare_fieldcat USING 'AIRPTO' '目的机场' 20.

" 显示ALV
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
EXPORTING
i_structure_name = 'SPFLI'
it_fieldcat = gt_fieldcat
TABLES
t_outtab = gt_data
EXCEPTIONS
program_error = 1
OTHERS = 2.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
WRITE: / 'Error in ALV display'.
ENDIF.

" 准备字段目录的子程序
FORM prepare_fieldcat USING p_fieldname p_coltext p_outputlen.
CLEAR gs_fieldcat.
gs_fieldcat-fieldname = p_fieldname.
gs_fieldcat-seltext_m = p_coltext.
gs_fieldcat-outputlen = p_outputlen.
APPEND gs_fieldcat TO gt_fieldcat.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: SAP Task Center’s New 3rd Party Integration
entry.content:
🌟Exciting Business Update: SAP Task Center Introduces Cutting-Edge Third-Party Integration Capability! 🌟

Businesses and partners, take note – SAP Task Center has just unveiled a groundbreaking new capability that has the potential to redefine task management for third-party applications. This highly anticipated feature empowers customers and partners to seamlessly build and connect task providers for non-SAP and legacy applications within the Task Center, ushering in a centralized platform for streamlined task management.  

In a strategic move towards offering enhanced flexibility and integration options, SAP Task Center now supports the much-coveted Third-Party scenario. This pivotal integration allows businesses to seamlessly connect other systems with their core business processes, creating a seamless workflow where tasks generated by third-party services are automatically synchronized with the SAP Task Center. With a unified task view, businesses can optimize resource allocation and boost productivity across multiple platforms, all while ensuring the security and reliability of sensitive business data through SAP's robust APIs.

 For those keen to delve deeper, our published adoption guide provides a comprehensive resource for interface and API implementation, as well as security and authentication set-up:

SAP Task Center Adoption Guide

SAP Task Center Documentation

Furthermore, while SAP Task Center's free standard plan already offers integration with supported SAP cloud solutions, our on-premise and third-party service plans open up a world of untapped potential. Explore the full range of options at SAP Discovery Center.

In a synergistic collaboration announced at SAP TechEd 2023, SAP has proudly partnered with SAP Partner ITCE, a leading Bulgarian consulting and training company operating across the Central and Eastern European (CEE) region. Our joint endeavor has yielded TaskConnect, a revolutionary solution developed in collaboration with SAP architects and technical consultants. TaskConnect serves as the essential bridge, offering seamless integration support for non-SAP service management systems and allowing businesses to effortlessly bring in IT service management tasks, such as incidents and approvals, directly into SAP Task Center.

 Are you ready to take your task management to the next level? Join us as we continue to redefine the boundaries of task management and integration, and unlock a world of unparalleled efficiency. Let's make it happen – together! #SAPTaskCenter #TaskConnect #InnovationAtAboveLimits #ThirdPartyIntegration #BusinessInnovation 🚀

Dim randomNumber As Integer

randomNumber = Int((9999 - 1000 + 1) * Rnd + 1000)

entry.title: Audit Data and Audit Logging in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Audit Data in SAP Commerce

What is Audit Data?

Audit data in SAP Commerce Cloud consists of database tables and their records that track every persistence action within the system, such as creation, modification, and deletion of entities. This historical record of changes is essential for various operational and regulatory needs.

Purpose of Audit Data

The primary purposes of audit data in Hybris are as follows:

1. Tracking Changes: It helps monitor who made changes to any data and when, ensuring the integrity and security of the system.

2. Troubleshooting: By providing a history of changes, audit data aids in diagnosing and resolving system issues or errors.

3. Compliance: Maintaining audit trails is a legal requirement in many industries. This is especially crucial for regulatory compliance, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in the European Union.

A Brief History of Audit Data Feature

SAP Commerce VersionImprovements
6.6Audit data capability introduced in SAP Hybris, and it has been enabled by default since its first release.
6.7SAP introduced more flexible configuration options, providing users with more granular control over which entities and attributes are audited.
1808Significant performance optimizations were made to the auditing process.
Recent versionsSupport for auditing additional entities, such as more complex custom item types, has been continuously expanded.

SAP Documentation on Audit Data

The following pages (and their sub-pages) from SAP documentation can be consulted to get more in-depth information about audit data:

  • Generic Data Report and Audit
  • Data Report and Audit

Enabling / Disabling of Audit Data

By default, auditing is enabled globally through the auditing.enabled=true property. To disable it, add auditing.enabled=false to your local.properties file.

Once auditing is globally enabled, we can then start enabling it for particular types, using the audit.<typecode>.enabled=true naming convention. For example, to enable auditing for the User type, use audit.user.enabled=true. There are many that are audited by default, as long as global auditing is enabled. We can see the list of such types in <HYBRIS_BIN_DIR>/platform/bin/platform/project.properties.

Conversely, we can use audit.<typecode>.enabled=false to disable auditing for some types where it is enabled out-of-the-box.

Refer to SAP knowledge base article 3387842 - Audit Tables in SAP Commerce Cloud for more information about the consequences of disabling auditing, as well as other details about auditing.

Storage of Audit Data in the Database

Names of Audit Tables

In SAP Hybris, you can easily identify the audit tables, because they all have the postfix of "sn", which stands for "snapshot". These tables store snapshot records of data changes, capturing the state of an entity at specific points in time.

The full convention for the audit table names is as follows:

<deploymentName><numericTypeCode>sn

For example, for products, we have the following out-of-the-box definition in core-items.xml:

 

<itemtype code="Product"
          extends="GenericItem"
          jaloclass="de.hybris.platform.jalo.product.Product"
          autocreate="true"
          generate="true">
    <deployment table="products" typecode="1" propertytable="ProductProps"/>
    <attributes>
        ...
    </attributes>
    <indexes>
        ...
    </indexes>
</itemtype>

 

As you can see, this entity type has the following main characteristics:

Deployment nameproducts
Type code1

Therefore, the audit table, which stores all insertions, modifications and deletions of products will be named products1sn.

Please note that audit tables are not part of the type system in SAP Commerce, so we cannot use modelService or flexible search queries to work with them. All the operations on these tables are done using plain JDBC out-of-the-box. However, there is a higher-level API available for querying and removing audit data. If you want to customize how auditing works, you will need to rely on that API as well as use JDBC.

Audit Table Structure

Each audit table has the same structure, as follows:

Column NameDescription
IDAudit record id.
ITEMPK
Audited instance PK: For example, if we are auditing users, it would be the user’s PK.
ITEMTYPEPKAudited type PK (ComposedType😞 All types in SAP Commerce are instances of ComposedType.Here, we provide the PK of that instance for the chosen type.
timestampThe timestamp of the change: Indicates when the change occurred to the underlying entity.
currenttimestamp
Audit record timestamp: Indicates the time at which the audit record itself was created. This is usually the same as, or within a few milliseconds of, the timestamp above.
changinguser
Author of the change.
context
Additional context (JSON): This could include, for example, the role of the user who made a change, the IP address, or the change reason.
payloadbefore

The whole business payload of the audited type before a persistence operation (JSON).

Before an INSERT, the payloadbefore is empty.

payloadafter

The whole business payload of the audited type after a persistence operation (JSON).

After a DELETE, the payloadafter is empty.

operationtype0 - deletion, 1 - creation, 2 - modification

Additional fields will be available if the audited type is a relationship - see SAP documentation in one of the earlier sections.

Example of an Audit Data Table

We will make a query to the products audit table. For simplicity we will just select all the data in the table. In real-life scenarios, we would typically apply filters.

SELECT * FROM products1sn;

Make sure to enter that query in the "SQL Query" tab in HAC, as opposed to the "Flexible Query" tab, since, as noted earlier, audit data tables are not Hybris entities.

 We will see something like the following as the result:

Refer to SAP knowledge base article 3351734 - How to enable auditing to track every persistence action and its payload change for items for more details and examples of querying audit data.

Working with Audit Data

Audit Data Programming

SAP provides various means to collect and create reports for audit data, but there are limited tools available to visually present it, besides those described in the above sections.

You can create audit configuration XML files, and generate reports in Java (details are available in the SAP documentation referenced in the previous chapters), but you will need to devise a method to present them to web users in the UI.

Personal Data Audit Reports

Purpose

Personal data reports have been a feature of SAP Commerce since version 1905.

This functionality allows for the auditing of changes to personal data, helping organizations track modifications and ensure compliance with data protection regulations such as GDPR.

Personal data reports use the audit data feature behind the scenes.

SAP Documentation

Detailed information about this feature can be found in the following SAP documentation pages: Personal Data Reporting Configuration in Backoffice Framework and Generating Personal Data Reports in Backoffice Framework.

Example

Personal data reports can be generated through the Backoffice framework in the "Customer Service" perspective.

To enable the "Customer Service" perspective, I added the following extensions to my vanilla accelerator (version 2211):

 

<extension name="backoffice"/>
<extension name="ticketsystem"/>
<extension name="customersupportbackoffice"/>

 

Then I re-initialized my system to import the initial data of those extensions into the database. If you are adding these extensions to a system that is already in production and cannot initialize it, then you will need to perform a system update and import the impexes from these extensions either manually or automatically (further explanation is outside the scope of this article).

Then I made sure that my user is part of the following groups:

  • customersupportagentgroup - Customer Support Agent Group
  • customersupportagentrole - Customer Support Agent Role

After that, I can either log in directly to the "Customer Support" perspective or log in to the Backoffice Administrative perspective and then switch to the "Customer Support" perspective, depending on whether my user also has the "Backoffice Administrator" role in addition to the above:

Once you are in the "Customer Support" perspective, choose "Customers" from the "Session Context", and then click on the customer you want to generate the personal data report for:

Once you are viewing the customer details, click on the button at the top to generate the personal data report about them:

If your system has been in production for some time, it may take some time for SAP Commerce to generate the report. Once it is generated, you will be able to see it when you go to the "Personal Data Reports" section, and then enter that user again to see their report:

The report is in the form of an HTML file, which is zipped and stored in the associated entity as a media item. You will need to download it to your local computer, unzip and open:

You can now view the user's report and expand and collapse the detailed sections:

Audit Data Cleanup

Out-Of-The-Box Cleanup Scenarios

The out-of-the-box platform provides some methods to remove audit data. These methods are located in the DefaultWriteAuditGateway class, specifically removeAuditRecordsForType(..) and removeAuditRecords(...).

These methods are currently only used for audit data removal in some out-of-the-box tests and a few other scenarios:

  • CommerceServicesCustomerCleanupHook: Removal of related entities when customer is removed; it actually removes many entities but does not remove that customer's audit data.
  • CommerceServicesOrderCleanupHook: Removal of related entities when an order is removed.
  • AuditServicesCustomerCleanupHook: Removal of personal data reports when a customer is removed.
  • CsTicketCleanupHook: Removal of ticket events when a ticket is removed.
  • WishlistCustomerCleanupHook: Removal of customer wishlists when a customer is removed.

Therefore, if you want to customize the logic for the classes above or remove related entities for other types when those objects are removed, you will need to implement the interface ItemCleanupHook<MODEL extends ItemModel>.

Regular Cleanup

If not regularly removed, audit data will quickly expand to occupy most of the space in your database. That's why SAP recommends disabling it immediately if you don't need it or implementing procedures to clean it up regularly according to your required filters.

To disable all audit data, set the property auditing.enabled=false.

SAP does not provide built-in functionality for regular audit data cleanup, so we recommend writing a cronjob to remove audit records older than a specific time, such as one month. For ideas, refer to the SAP forum thread How to clean-up audit table records older than 30 days on SAP Commerce Cloud v2? and SAP article 2793188 - [How To] Audit Clean up Job.

You can also customize your cronjob to set different retention periods for various types of audit records, such as a year for customers, six months for products, and a month for everything else.

You can extend the DefaultWriteAuditGateway class to add more logic (using JDBC), enabling your cronjob to rely on it for audit record removal. As a reminder, audit records are not Hybris items, so you will need to use JDBC to remove them directly from the database.

For more information on audit data cleanup and other kinds of cleanup in SAP Hybris, refer to the SAP blog articles: Data Maintenance and Cleanup of a SAP Commerce Cloud Project and 3078172 - Tables that are recommended to be purged regularly.

One-Time Full Cleanup

When you start implementing audit data cleanup and the project has been in operation for some time, there is probably a lot of data in audit tables, and cleaning it up using the cronjob above will take too long. Therefore, you will need to either truncate audit tables (those postfixed with "sn") or remove them altogether (if you opt to disable auditing). After you do that, you will need to restart Hybris so it updates its caches.

Refer to SAP article 3453806 - How to check and DELETE entries from audit tables - SAP Commerce for step-by-step instructions on performing a one-time deletion of audit data. However, I recommend using "truncate" instead of "delete," as this operation will be faster.

Audit Logging

What is Audit Logging?

Audit logging is another facet of auditing in SAP Commerce. No data is written to the database, as it is designed to display detailed information in the log about various events.

In a fresh installation of the accelerator (before auditing is disabled), you can see many messages like "DB_AUDIT", "session user changed," or "session closed" from Slf4jAuditableActionHandler in your log. This is from audit logging.

Out-of-the-box, Hybris audits a variety of events and prints them to the log. The types of events and the information on how you can utilize audit logging for your own purposes are described in the SAP documentation: Audit Logging.

Enabling / Disabling Audit Logging

Since audit logging is just log messages, to disable it, simply add these settings in your local.properties:

 

log4j2.logger.auditableActionsHandler.name=de.hybris.platform.audit.actions.impl.Slf4jAuditableActionHandler
log4j2.logger.auditableActionsHandler.level=OFF

 

What is "DB Logging"?

The term "DB logging" can be confusing for someone new to audit logging. Is it keeping any data in the database? Is it the actual RDBMS specific database logging?

SAP explains that, unlike Generic Audit, audit logging does not store the content of database operations. It only records the create, update, and delete operation types, modified item PKs, type PKs, and standard audit logging elements such as user names and timestamps.

SAP also states that the logic is implemented in the de.hybris.platform.persistence.audit.DBAuditHandler interface with the default implementation in de.hybris.platform.persistence.audit.impl.DefaultDBAuditHandler. Reviewing these classes (and any they reference) reveals that nothing is written to the database, it is purely for logging.

It logs every database operation to the log file, which can be helpful for tracing and troubleshooting.

Enabling / Disabling DB Logging

DB logging is not supposed to be activated in a local installation, and there is a property for this that is set to false by default:

 

dbaudit.disabled.for.standalone=false

 

With this property enabled, DB audit is only supposed to activate in the cloud environment.

However, I believe SAP Commerce currently has a bug where DB audit is still activating in the local environment. Every time I install a fresh instance of Hybris, I see lots of DB_AUDIT messages in my log.

While writing this article, I debugged this issue. It checks if DB audit should be enabled in the DefaultDBAuditEnablementService out-of-the-box class, method calculateDBAuditDisabled(). Essentially, three conditions must be met for it to disable DB logging:

  1. The config property "dbaudit.disabled.for.standalone" should be set to true.
  2. It must not be a cloud environment (checks properties specific to the cloud).
  3. The Registry class must indicate running in standalone mode or as a test tenant (determined by the name "junit").

As a result, it incorrectly determines that we are not running in standalone mode. Therefore, even with the property "dbaudit.disabled.for.standalone" set to true, DB logging still occurs locally.

The only solution I have found so far is to disable DB logging for each individual type, which you can do as follows:

 

dbaudit.processtask.disabled=true

 

Note that processtask is the name of the type, and it should be written in all lowercase.

Because you are making this modification in your local.properties, you should rebuild and restart your server afterward.

SAP knowledge base article 3342126 - How to disable DB_AUDIT records in the logs explains how to get the names of the types that need to be disabled for the purposes of DB logging.

Conclusion

Audit data in SAP Commerce Cloud is a useful component for ensuring regulatory compliance and facilitating troubleshooting. Introduced in version 6.6, the feature has evolved with more granular control, performance optimizations, and support for additional entities. Despite the robust capabilities, managing and cleaning up audit data is essential to prevent excessive database growth and maintain system performance. Understanding how to enable, configure, and utilize audit data effectively, along with employing regular cleanup practices, ensures that businesses can leverage audit data efficiently.

entry.title: Convert CSV File to XML Format via SFTP in PI/PO Without Using Code
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope everything is all right and well. Since I was a junior, we have been asked many times by our customers to perform services that change file formats. Even though they are similar services, I had to find alternative solutions because the methods I used sometimes did not work. In some services, I had to combine different methods or services. In this scenario, I will show you how to convert CSV file sent via SFTP to XML format. Unlike the previous examples, this time we will create ESR objects, but we will not write any code as in the previous examples.

 

There are some points I want to mention here :

  • Our service is an asynchronous service.
  • No special method or code was used when creating ESR objects.
  • There is no special setting used in the mapping section, all fields are mapped exactly

For example, let's say we have a CSV file named Units with the file content as follows:

We use message transform beans to convert our CSV file to XML format. You can find detailed technical information about this on Mr. Engswee's blog :

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/fcc-parameter-reference-for-messagetransformbean-module/ba-p/13250368

First of all, what we need to do is to create ESR objects.The first important part is to create a Data Type :

The second important part is that while service interfaces are defined, they must be asynchronous:

After the ESR objects are created, we need an SFTP Sender channel that can convert the CSV format to into XML structure.

After entering SFTP information, ASMA settings are made as follows:

Here, we go directly to the module tab, just like in the other examples we have done. By entering the following parameters, we will perform XML conversions:

Below you can find the modules and parameters we used:

 

localejbs/Custom_AF_Modules/MessageTransformBean                Local Enterprise Bean     Plain2XML

localejbs/CallSapAdapter                                                                 Local Enterprise Bean     entry

 

Plain2XML  Transform.Class                                    com.sap.aii.messaging.adapter.Conversion 

Plain2XML  Transform.ContentType                         text/xml;charset=utf-8 

Plain2XML  xml.conversionType                              SimplePlain2XML 

Plain2XML  xml.documentName                             MT_TEST 

Plain2XML  xml.documentNamespace                    http://namespace/TEMPTEST

Plain2XML  xml.documentsSkipFirstRows              1 

Plain2XML  xml.endSeparator                                'nl' 

Plain2XML  xml.fieldNames  Necropolis,Inferno,Castle,Dungeon,Stronghold,Tower,Conflux,Fortress,Rampart,Neutral

Plain2XML  xml.fieldSeparator                                   ; 

Plain2XML  xml.processFieldNames                         fromConfiguration 

Plain2XML  xml.structureTitle                                    Record

 

After the procedures here, our file will be converted to XML format. After these steps, Integrated Configuration is created.

 

There are some points I want to mention here :

  • If we set the xml.processFieldNames parameter fromConfiguration, we have to use the xml.fieldNames parameter.
  • In the xml.fieldNames section, we enter the field names in the CSV file here. Field names must be in their original order in the CSV file to avoid shifting values.
  • When you open the CSV file with notepad, you will see which character separate the fields. We specify that character in the xml.fieldSeparator section. In this example, our character is a semicolon.
  • We used the value 'nl' for the xml.endSeperator field. nl stands for new line. Quotation marks must be used.

 

Thus, we can easily convert CSV file format to XML data without using any code.

Thank you, have a great day!

entry.title: Flexible Real Estate - Accrual Engine Legacy Migration Technique
entry.content:
Introduction

 

SAP Real Estate Management module, is a part of the comprehensive SAP ERP suite, stands out as a powerful tool designed to address the complex needs of real estate professionals. This module integrates seamlessly with other SAP functionalities, providing a robust platform for managing property portfolios, lease agreements, and facility operations. However, one critical limitation that users must be aware of, is its incompatibility with the Accrual Engine Legacy Migration for Real Estate contracts.

This document delves into the implications of this limitation, providing insights into how organizations can overcome the challenges associated with migrating real estate contracts within SAP.

 

Real Estate Accrual Engine Legacy Migration Limitation

 

Leasing Contracts that have more than one month rental period, Paid or collected in advance , shall have Rent amortization entries, these entries help in the systematic allocation of rent expenses over the lease term, ensuring that the financial statements accurately reflect the true financial position and performance of the organization by spreading the cost/revenue of a lease over the rental period.

 

Kindly check the below business example, explaining the issue using SAP terminology

Fiscal year Start =  01.07

Cutoff Date= 31.03.2024

Contract start date=01.02.2020

Condition Start Date (Current Rental Period)= 01.02.2024

Condition Amount =9000

Frequency = 3 Months- In advance

Deferral Balance =6000

Deferral Remaining amount = 3000

 

According to SAP, Accrual Engine Legacy data transfer for the above business example is not supported by RE-FX and the Legacy Data Transfer Customizing view V_TACEDS_LEGACYM can not be maintained in the RE environment and that handling of legacy accrual/deferral data is only supported by RE-FX when migrating from RE Classic.

Also one of SAP recommendation is to ensure that the opening balance for respective accrual/deferral accounts is zero at the point when starting with the RE-FX accrual/deferral, which practically will not be applicable to have accrual/deferral  accounts zero for all leasing contracts 

 

Accrual Engine Legacy Migration Technique

 

Based on the above limitation, we used a workaround technique, to be able to migrate Accrual Deferral amounts in Real Estate Module

 

Rental period within one Fiscal year -  2024

Fiscal year Start =  01.07

Cutoff Date= 31.03.2024

Contract start date=01.02.2020

Condition Start Date (Current Rental Period)= 01.02.2024

Condition Amount =9000

Frequency = 3 Months- In advance

Deferral Balance =6000

Deferral Remaining amount = 3000

  • Accrual / Deferral Date:

“Cash Flow” Date should be = 01.02.2024 – “Start of the Rental Period”

Then we need to execute “Accrual / Deferral”  run – T-Code: RERAALCN and   Key date should be the same as  the cutoff date : 31.03.2023

Cash Flow Rule (Rental period across One Fiscal year) : Date should be the start of rental period

  • The below accounting entries will be generated for the Accrual / Deferral run in 31.03.2024

 

1

Dr 1* Prepaid  Rent                9000

Cr 3* Rent Expense        9000

Inception Entry - I

2

Dr 3* Rent Expense        6000

Cr 1* Prepaid  Rent                6000

Periodic Recognition Entry- P

 

  •               Based on the above Accrual / Deferral Run entries  , Prepaid rent GL account will have correct balance , but regarding  Rent Expense / or  Revenue accounts we will still have delta amounts for the pervious rental periods in the current fiscal year, which should be uploaded using LTMC or whatever uploading tool. 

 

 

Now let’s see the above example applied on SAP Flexible Real estate Module

Create Lease In Real Estate Contract

Fiori App

Manage Contract

Menu Path

SAP Menu è Accounting è Flexible Real Estate Management è Contract è Process Contract

T-Code

 RECN

 

 

Rent Frequency = 3 Months

 

Rent Condition Amount = 9000

Current Rental Period From 01.02.2024 to 30.04.2024

 

Cash Flow From= 01.02.2024 (Start of the Rental Period – Condition Valid From Date)

 

As part of the Real Estate Migration Steps , execute the Accrual / Deferral run on the Cut Off Date, 31.03.2024

Fiori App

Accrual/Deferral: Contracts

Menu Path

SAP Menu è Accounting è Flexible Real Estate Management è Accounting è Accrual/Deferral è Accrual/Deferral: Contracts

T-Code

 RERAALCN

 

Accrual / Deferral Run Accounting entries

Deferral Balance , and Deferral remaining Balance

 

Fiori App

Display Accrual Objects

Menu Path

SAP Menu è Accounting è Flexible Real Estate Management è Accounting è Accrual/Deferral è Display Accrual Objects

T-Code

 REEXACRSHOW

 

 

Now let’s see more complex example when Rental period is across two years and we need to post to the Prepaid GL account (Balance Sheet GL) , the amount related to current Fiscal Year only.

Rental period across Two Fiscal years 2023 / 2024

Fiscal year Start =  01.07

Cutoff Date= 31.03.2024

Contract start date=01.05.2020

Condition Start Date (Current Rental Period)= 01.05.2023

Condition Amount =12000

Frequency = 12 Months- In advance

Balance =11000

Remaining amount = 1000

  • Accrual / Deferral Date:

“Cash Flow” Date should be = 01.07.2023

Then we need to execute “Accrual / Deferral” with the cut off date : 28.02.2024

Cash Flow Rule (Rental period across Two Fiscal years) : Date should be the start of fiscal year (01.07)

  • The below accounting entries will be generated for the Accrual / Deferral run in 31.03.2024

 

1

Dr 1* Prepaid  Rent                10000

Cr 3* Rent Expense        10000

Inception Entry - I

2

Dr 3* Rent Expense        9000

Cr 1* Prepaid  Rent                9000

Periodic Recognition Entry- P

 

Now let’s see the above example applied on SAP Flexible Real estate Module

 

Create Lease In Real Estate Contract

Fiori App

Manage Contract

Menu Path

SAP Menu è Accounting è Flexible Real Estate Management è Contract è Process Contract

T-Code

 RECN

 

 

 

Cash Flow From= 01.07.2023 (Start of Fiscal Year Date)

Rent Frequency = 12 Months

Rent Condition Amount = 12000

Current Rental Period From 01.07.2023 (Cash Flow From Date) to 30.04.2024

As part of the Real Estate Migration Steps , execute the Accrual / Deferral run on the Cut Off Date, 31.03.2024

Fiori App

Accrual/Deferral: Contracts

Menu Path

SAP Menu è Accounting è Flexible Real Estate Management è Accounting è Accrual/Deferral è Accrual/Deferral: Contracts

T-Code

 RERAALCN

 

Accrual / Deferral Run Accounting entries

 

Deferral Balance , and Deferral remaining Balance

Fiori App

Display Accrual Objects

Menu Path

SAP Menu è Accounting è Flexible Real Estate Management è Accounting è Accrual/Deferral è Display Accrual Objects

T-Code

 REEXACRSHOW

 

 

 

Disclaimer and Liability Notice

This document may discuss sample coding or other information that does not include SAP official interfaces and therefore is not supported by SAP. Changes made based on this information are not supported and can be overwritten during an upgrade.

SAP will not be held liable for any damages caused by using or misusing the information, code or methods suggested in this document, and anyone using these methods does so at his/her own risk.

SAP offers no guarantees and assumes no responsibility or liability of any type with respect to the content of this technical article or code sample, including any liability resulting from incompatibility between the content within this document and the materials and services offered by SAP. You agree that you will not hold, or seek to hold, SAP responsible or liable with respect to the content of this document.

SAP Real Estate Management  FIN Real Estate

entry.title: Conversion factor for alternate UOM in material master
entry.content:
Hi, recently i faced an issue regarding alternate UOM and conversion factor. I am sharing some insights from my experience.

Problem Statement: There are two manufacturing plants, semi-finished material is manufactured in one plant location for example A and send to another plant location for example B by stock transfer. The problem came in the units of measure. The material base units of measure is in NOS and the UOM required in Delivery Challan (Stock transfer)is in KG.

Solution: The conversion factor is added in material master additional data tab as per below screenshot:

Concept of alternate UOM in SAP:

A unit of measure that is defined in the SAP system in addition to the base unit of measure. For example, the base unit of measure might be "NOS" while the alternative unit might be "carton" or "pallet".

In above screenshot X is denominator and Y is numerator:

To convert a quantity, whose unit of measure is not the same as the base unit of measure, into the base unit of measure, the system requires a quotient:

Quantity (in alternative unit of measure) = quotient * quantity (in base unit of measure)

For example: The alternative unit of measure is kilogram (kg). The base unit of measure is in numbers(Nos). 0.468 kg correspond to 1 Nos.

0.468 Kg = 1 Nos => 1 KG = 1/0.468 Nos

Here the numerator is 1 and denominator is 0.486

You can also enter only whole numbers in the numerator and denominator field, i.e if 0.468 kg correspond to 1 Nos, you must enter integer multiples (468 KG = 1000 Nos). In this case the numerator is 468. and numerator is 1000. You can add more than one alternative units, the system converts all other units into base units of measure.

entry.title: Efficient Data Retrieval and UI Binding To Value Help: Using Batch Requests with OData in SAPUI5
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have observed that there is limited content available regarding the process of sending batch requests from SAPUI5 applications to OData services, receiving the response, and subsequently binding the retrieved data to a Combo Box for value help

So in this Blog I would like to explain create a Combo Box using XML, Send a batch request from SAP UI5 and Bind the data to Combo Box for Value Help.

What is a batch Request?

In SAP OData, a batch request is a mechanism for sending multiple HTTP requests as a single batch to the OData service endpoint. Instead of sending individual HTTP requests for each operation, such as read, create, update, or delete, you can group multiple operations into a single batch request.

Step1- Creating Combo Box for drop down using XML

 

<ComboBox id = 'idProductType' items="{path: '/'}" > " Path should be "/"
<items>
"product_type and product_name should be your entity type Properties from OData service 
<core:Item key="{product_type}" text="{product_name}"></core:Item>
</items>
</ComboBox>

 

 

 

Step2- ODATA Entity Set 

Step-3 -Populating the data for Drop Down Values

Step4- Sending the BATCH request from UI5 Application.

 

var oModel = new sap.ui.model.odata.v2.ODataModel("Service URL"); "Service URL will be the same configured in Manifest.Json

                var entitySet = ["/Your Entity Set name from ODATA"];
                var prodcutType = [ ];

                var oComboBox = this.byId("idProductType"); "Id from XML view

                var onSuccess = function (oData) {
                    console.log("Request successful", oData);
                    var aResponses = oData.results;
                    for (var i = 0; i < aResponses.length; i++) {
                        var oResponseData = aResponses[i];
                        productType = productType.concat(oResponseData);
                    }
                }

                "Create Json model for Combo Box
                var oModelComboBox = new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel();
                oComboBox.setModel(oModelComboBox);

                oModelComboBox.setData(productType);

                "Error if Batch Request Fails
                var onError = function (oError) {
                    console.error("Error:", oError);
                };

                var mParameters = {
                    batchGroupId: "myBatchGroup",
                    success: onSuccess,
                    error: onError
                };
                oModel.read(entitySet, mParameters);
                oModel.submitChanges(mParameters);

Best Regards,

Thulasiram

entry.title: How to Generate and Download XML on the fly without persisting in CAP Application(Node.js)
entry.content:
Use Case:

When the user clicks the "XML" button, the application will fetch the account details and payment information from the database. It will then prepare an XML file. This XML file will be downloaded and stored in the "downloads" folder on the user's device.

The screenshot below shows the CAP application UI, which includes a custom button to trigger XML generation and download.

The downloaded XML looks like the screenshot below.

Implementation Steps:

  • Define a Virtual field in service
  • Handler logic to generate and download xml
    • Importing necessary modules
    • Implementing the Read Handler
    • Preparing the JSON Content
    • Converting the JSON to XML
    • Converting XML data to buffer to download
  • Converting “Open file” link to a button in the UI

Define a Virtual field in service:

Define a virtual field at the service level with type “LargeBinary” and media type “application/xml”.

entity AccountStatements {
virtual null    as   xml : LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: 'application/xml',
}

This will automatically generate a hyperlink labelled “Open File” in the UI as shown in the screenshot below.

Handler logic to generate and download xml:

To download an account statement in XML format, first, retrieve the XML data for the specified account statement ID using a database transaction. Then, convert the XML data to a buffer. Finally, set the file name based on the account statement ID and specify the content type as XML.

Importing Necessary Modules

To handle the conversion and streaming of the XML content, you'll require the Buffer and Readable modules from Node.js. Import these modules into your project with the following code:

import { Buffer } from "buffer";
import { Readable } from "stream";

Add the “xml2js” library through package.json

Import the library to proceed the xml conversion

import xml2js from "xml2js";

Implementing the Read Handler:

In the Read handler of the entity, verify if the URL path includes "xml". (name of the virtual field defined in your entity). If you have named the virtual field differently, ensure to check for that specific name in the URL.

virtual null                as xml                  : LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: 'application/xml',

If the URL path contains the specified virtual field name(url.includes(“xml”)), proceed to generate the XML content. Convert this XML content into buffer data, push the buffered data into a Readable stream, and return it. Make sure to include the media content type and the disposition filename in the response.

Example:

/dch/ui/payment/accountstatements/AccountStatements(${object.ID})/xml

Preparing the JSON content :

Construct the json content with the retrieved data from the database.

private buildAccountStatementJson(
    accStmtEntries: Camt053Ntry[],
    AccountStatement: RetrieveAccountStatementDetails
  ) {
    const jsoncontent = {
      $: {
        xmlns: "urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:camt.053.001.12",
        "xmlns:xsi": "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance",
        "xsi:schemaLocation": "urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:camt.053.001.12 camt.053.001.12.xsd"
      },
      BkToCstmrStmt: {
        GrpHdr: {
          MsgId: String(AccountStatement.accountStatementDisplayId),
          CreDtTm: AccountStatement.accountStatementCreatedAt
        },
        Stmt: {
          Id: String(AccountStatement.accountStatementDisplayId),
          LglSeqNb: String(AccountStatement.accountStatementDisplayId),
          CreDtTm: AccountStatement.accountStatementCreatedAt,
          Acct: {
            Id: {
              Othr: {
                Id: AccountStatement.accountDisplayId
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    };
    return jsoncontent;
  }

Converting the JSON to XML:

Convert the JSON content to XML format by passing the JSON data to the xml2js library function, enabling the download of the data as an XML file.

private jsonToCamt053Xml(jsondata: Camt053Document) {
    const builder = new xml2js.Builder({
      headless: true,
      renderOpts: {
        pretty: false // Set to true for pretty formatting
      }
    });
    let camt053Xml = {
      Document: jsondata
    };
    camt053Xml = builder.buildObject(camt053Xml);
    return camt053Xml;
  }

Converting XML data to buffer to download:

Adding the XML data to a buffer is necessary because buffers handle raw binary data, which is useful for file operations such as downloading. Converting the XML string to a buffer ensures that the data is correctly encoded and can be managed efficiently for creating the downloadable file. This step is crucial for maintaining data integrity during the download process.

        BufferData = Buffer.from(xmlData.xmlString, "utf8");
        fileName = `AccountStatement${xmlData.accountStatementDisplayId}.xml`;
        contentType = "application/xml";

Converting “Open File” link to a button in the UI:

 Define a UI Fragment that contains a button with the handler in app/webapp/ext/fragment section.

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns="sap.m">
    <Button
        core:require="{ handler: 'sap/erp4sme/c4b/payment/accountstatements/ext/fragment/DownloadXML'}"
        text="XML"
        press="handler.onPress" />
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Create a handler for the button that, when pressed, displays a toast message saying "DownloadingXML" and then redirects the browser to the XML URL.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], function (MessageToast) {
    'use strict';
    return {
        onPress: function (oEvent) {
            const view = this.editFlow.getView();
            const i18nModelBundle = view.getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle();
            MessageToast.show(i18nModelBundle.getText("DownloadingXml"));
            const object = oEvent.getSource().getBindingContext().getObject();
            sap.m.URLHelper.redirect(`/dch/ui/payment/accountstatements/AccountStatements(${object.ID})/xml`, true);
        }
    };
});

Add the fragment in the columns section in manifest.json.

                   "DownloadXML": {
                      "position": {
                        "anchor": "DataField::createdAt",
                        "placement": "After"
                      },
                      "header": "{i18n>XML}",
                      "template": "sap.erp4sme.c4b.payment.accountstatements.ext.fragment.DownloadXML",
                      "horizontalAlign": "Center",
                      "width": "5em"
                    },

entry.title: How to Generate and Download CSV on the fly without persisting in CAP Application(Node.js)
entry.content:
Use Case:

When the user clicks the "CSV" button, the application will fetch the accounting document details and it will then prepare a CSV file. This CSV file will then be downloaded and stored in the local filesystem on the user's device.

The screenshot below shows the CAP application UI, which includes a custom button to trigger CSV generation and download.

The downloaded CSV looks like the screenshot below:

Implementation Steps:

  • Define a Virtual field in service
  • Handler logic to generate and download csv
    • Importing necessary modules
    • Implementing the Read Handler
    • Preparing the JSON Content
    • Converting the JSON to CSV
    • Converting CSV data to buffer to download
  • Converting “Open file” link to a button in the UI

Define a Virtual field in service: Define a virtual field at the service level with type “LargeBinary” and media type “application/csv”.

entity Documents {
    key ID                  : UUID;
        name                : String;
        virtual null as csv : LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: 'application/csv',
}

 

This will automatically generate a hyperlink labelled “Open File” in the UI as shown in the screenshot below.

Handler logic to generate and download csv:

To download an accounting document in CSV format, first, retrieve the accounting Document with Book and accounting Principle  details for the specified document ID using a database transaction. Then, convert the csv data to a buffer. Finally, set the file name based on the accounting document ID and specify the content type as CSV.

Importing Necessary Modules

To handle the conversion and streaming of the CSV content, you'll require the Buffer and Readable modules from Node.js and json2csv library to convert the Json data to csv format. Import these modules into your project with the following code:

import { Buffer } from "buffer";
import { Readable } from "stream";
import { json2csv } from "json-2-csv";

Add the “json2csv” library through package.json

  "dependencies": {
    "json-2-csv": "^5.5.1",

Implementing the Read Handler:

In the Read handler of the entity, verify if the URL path includes "csv". (name of the virtual field defined in your entity). If you have named the virtual field differently, ensure to check for that specific name in the URL.

  virtual null                as csv                  : LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: 'application/csv',

If the URL path contains the specified virtual field name(url.includes(“csv”)), proceed to generate the CSV content. Convert this CSV content into buffer data, push the buffered data into a Readable stream, and return it. Make sure to include the media content type and the disposition filename in the response.

Example:

/dch/ui/accounting/documents/Documents(${object.ID})/csv

Preparing the JSON content :

Construct the JSON content with the retrieved data from the database.

let documentOutputValue: RetrieveDocumentDetails = {

      "Entity Type": EntityTypeCodes.ACCOUNTING_DOCUMENT,
      ID: documentsHeaderData.displayId.toString(),
      Company: companyData.name,
      "Accounting Principle": accountingPrincipleData.code,
      "Key Date": documentsHeaderData.CutOffDate,
      "Created On": documentsHeaderData.createdAt,
      "Created By": documentsHeaderData.createdBy,
      "Item ID": "",
      "Item Type": "",
      "Amount (Position Currency)": "",
      "Position Currency": "",
      "Amount (Local Currency)": "",
      "Local Currency": "",
      Text: "SAP Digital Currency Hub"
    };

Converting the JSON to CSV:

Convert the JSON content to CSV format by passing the JSON data to the json2csv library function, enabling the download of the data as a file.

  //Prepare csv output file
          const csvOptions = {
            delimiter: {
              wrap: '"',
              field: ";", // specify the delimiter here
              eol: "\n"
            }
          };
          const csvOutput = json2csv(documentOutput, csvOptions);

Converting CSV data to buffer to download:

Adding the CSV data to a buffer is necessary because buffers handle raw binary data, which is useful for file operations such as downloading. Converting the CSV data to a buffer ensures that the data is correctly encoded and can be managed efficiently for creating the downloadable file. This step is crucial for maintaining data integrity during the download process.

        BufferData = Buffer.from(csvOutput, "utf8");
          fileName = `AccountingDocument${documentsHeaderData.displayId}.csv`;
          contentType = "application/csv";
          return this.returnBufferContents(BufferData, fileName, contentType);

Converting “Open File” link to a button in the UI:

Define a UI Fragment that contains a button with the handler in app/webapp/ext/fragment section.

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns="sap.m">
    <Button
        core:require="{ handler: 'sap/erp4sme/c4b/accounting/documents/ext/fragment/ActionCsvDownload'}"
        text="CSV" press="handler.onPress" />
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Create a handler for the button that, when pressed, displays a toast message saying "DownloadingCSV" and then redirects the browser to the CSV URL.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], function (MessageToast) {
    'use strict';
    return {
        onPress: function (oEvent) {
            const view = this.editFlow.getView();
            const i18nModelBundle = view.getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle();
            MessageToast.show(i18nModelBundle.getText("DownloadingCSV"));
            const object = oEvent.getSource().getBindingContext().getObject();
            sap.m.URLHelper.redirect(`/dch/ui/accounting/documents/Documents(${object.ID})/csv`, true);
        }
    };
});

Add the fragment in the columns section in manifest.json.

                  "ActionCsvDownload": {
                                            "header": "{i18n>downloadcsv}",
                                            "position": {
                                                "anchor": "DataFieldForAction::DocumentsUiService.unconfirmPosting",
                                                "placement": "After"
                                            },
                                            "template": "sap.erp4sme.c4b.accounting.documents.ext.fragment.ActionCsvDownload",
                                            "horizontalAlign": "Center",
                                            "width": "5em"
                                        }

entry.title: How to Export MR11 List to an MS Excel File
entry.content:
This brief blog post will demonstrate how to export an MR11 list to an MS Excel file.

Issue

In MR11, there is no direct option to save the list to an MS Excel file via: Menu > System > List > Save > …

SAP Note 3229632 presents a solution, but I was unable to find the specified option in S/4HANA. 

Solution

Step 1: Right-click on the blue area of the MR11 list and select the "Local File" option. Note: There is a menu option labeled "Spreadsheet…", but it may not function correctly.

Step 2: In the popup window, choose the "Spreadsheet" option and click "Continue".

Step 3: The system will automatically generate a file name. You may modify the name if necessary. Then, click "Export to…" and save the file to your chosen directory.

SAP Notes

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003229632
